title: Bread & Puppet
excerpt: Peter Schumann’s family fled their home in Silesia, Germany during allied bombings in World War II. Living in a refugee camp, they survived on turnips and his mother’s sourdough rye bread. It was a life lesson in the terrors of war that, Peter points out, is a reality many North Americans have never experienced. Elka Schumann, born in Magnitogorsk to a Russian mother and American father — a radical journalist critical of Stalin — remembers living under constant fear of German bombings. Elka and Peter met in Munich when she was a student and he was in hospital with a head injury from a bicycle accident during a recruitment stunt for his experimental dance troupe. Based in a small village outside Munich, in an old medieval domicile for priests, the troupe would tour Germany showcasing their dance and woodcuts.
